assumptions: The developer can use a browser for YugabyteDB Aeon or Docker for a local cluster.
No Aeon account, Sandbox cluster, or local container is assumed to exist yet.
No payment method is assumed for the Sandbox path, which docs say requires no credit card.

developer_goal: Reach a usable YSQL shell against a free YugabyteDB cluster using the vendor's primary getting-started route.

first_success.outcome: A YSQL shell session is open against a running free cluster.
first_success.observable_signal: The shell shows a ysqlsh prompt such as yugabyte=> or yugabyte=#.

route_selection.surface: YugabyteDB Aeon Cloud Portal self-service web, with local Docker as a documented alternate
route_selection.rule: Journeys begin at account creation when a self-service cloud path exists; Aeon docs call Sandbox the quickest start, but signup fields are not publicly reconstructable and local Docker is a complete no-account alternate.

candidates.name: YugabyteDB Aeon signup, Sandbox cluster, Cloud Shell YSQL
candidates.reason: Official Aeon quick start calls this the quickest path and documents Sandbox plus Cloud Shell, but signup form fields, verification, and first-login tutorial screens are not enumerated.

candidates.name: YugabyteDB Aeon signup then 15-minute Get Started tutorial
candidates.reason: Docs say first login offers a guided tutorial that connects, loads sample data, and explores a sample app, without publishing ordered UI steps or a completion signal.

candidates.name: Local Docker quick start to ysqlsh
candidates.reason: Fully documented pull, run, status, and ysqlsh commands without an account, but it bypasses the account-creation boundary used for managed peers and is marked for advanced Docker users.

candidates.name: Dedicated cluster or desktop client shell
candidates.reason: Requires billing or trial access and additional client or topology configuration after account access.

required_path.action: Sign up for YugabyteDB Aeon if the selected route is the managed Sandbox path.

required_fields.label: Aeon signup fields and agreements
required_fields.notes: Docs link signup but do not list required inputs; public signup shell does not expose the form without client-side app execution.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can log in to YugabyteDB Aeon.

required_path.action: Create a Sandbox cluster with name, cloud provider, region, compatibility options, IP allow list, and downloaded admin credentials.

required_fields.label: Current IP address allow list entry

required_path.observable_result: Aeon provisions the Sandbox cluster and shows the cluster Overview when ready.

required_path.action: Launch Cloud Shell, confirm database and user, and enter the admin password to reach ysqlsh.
required_path.observable_result: Cloud Shell displays the ysqlsh prompt.

prerequisites.description: Docker runtime is required only if the local Docker candidate is selected instead of Aeon.

external_gates.description: Managed Sandbox path requires completing Aeon signup and login before Create Cluster.

unavoidable_waits.description: Sandbox provisioning takes around five minutes after Create Cluster.
Cloud Shell can take up to about thirty seconds to become ready.

platform_outcomes.description: Aeon bootstraps and provisions the Sandbox cluster and configures YugabyteDB.
Cloud Shell opens a browser ysqlsh session against the cluster.

excluded.reason: Documented as optional while waiting for the cluster.

excluded.item: Sample application language tutorials after Cloud Shell
excluded.reason: Begin after a cluster and CA or IP prerequisites beyond first shell access.

excluded.item: Reading Explore YSQL after local Docker connect
excluded.reason: Occurs after the ysqlsh prompt on the local route.

sources.title: YugabyteDB Aeon signup
evidence_supported: Unauthenticated static response does not enumerate form fields

sources.title: YugabyteDB Quick start for Docker
evidence_supported: Docker pull run status and ysqlsh commands

uncertainties.question: What exact fields, agreements, verification, and login methods appear in self-service Aeon signup?
uncertainties.impact: Without signup evidence the account-creation boundary for the managed route cannot be counted or verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated fresh-account screenshots or a read-only walkthrough of cloud.yugabyte.com/signup through first login.

uncertainties.question: Should the atlas route prefer Aeon Sandbox with account creation or the fully documented local Docker path?
uncertainties.impact: Cloud versus local changes whether account creation applies, action count, waits, and first-success surface.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment applying SELECTION-POLICY account-creation and self-service-web rules against the complete Docker alternate.

uncertainties.question: If Aeon is selected, is first success the Cloud Shell prompt or completion of the first-login 15-minute Get Started tutorial?
uncertainties.impact: The tutorial adds unknown screens and sample-data steps beyond Cloud Shell.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official enumeration or a recorded tutorial completion signal.
